title: SQM — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue -32.5% YoY; EBITDA -48.2% YoY; net income -45.1% YoY. EBITDA margin 25%.
aiSummary: Weak quarter driven entirely by lithium carbonate price collapse. Revenue fell 32.5% YoY despite stable lithium volumes because spot and contract prices are down ~45% YoY. EBITDA margin compressed from ~37% to 25% — still industry-leading but the trajectory is concerning if prices don't stabilize. The key unknown is SQM's ongoing contract renegotiation with Codelco for the Atacama salar — terms could structurally reset the cost base. At current lithium prices, SQM trades on cyclical trough valuation. Recovery depends on battery supply chain normalization and EV demand acceleration in China.
keyPoints: Revenue: CLP 780,000MM (-32.5% YoY); primary driver is lithium price, not volume
EBITDA margin 25.0% vs 37.5% prior year — significant compression
Net income: CLP 115,000MM (-45.1% YoY)
Watch: Atacama contract renegotiation terms with Codelco
Lithium carbonate spot ~USD 12,800/t; recovery contingent on EV demand

title: Santander Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +8.2% YoY; net income +11.5% YoY. Clean quarter — NIM expansion, stable credit quality.
aiSummary: Strong and clean quarter. Net income grew 11.5% YoY on NIM expansion driven by loan repricing at still-elevated real rates. Cost of risk held stable — no early signs of credit deterioration despite moderate consumer pressure. Fee income was flat YoY, which bears watching as rate cuts compress NII. Santander Chile's advantage vs peers is its lower wholesale funding reliance, which limits NIM sensitivity on the liability side. Near-term risk: further BCCh cuts will compress NIM; management guidance of moderate deceleration into H2 is the base case. Stock remains a quality compounder in Chilean banking.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 195,000MM (+11.5% YoY) — clean beat
NIM expansion on loan repricing; credit quality stable
Fee income flat YoY — watch as primary offset to NIM compression from rate cuts
Lower wholesale funding base limits liability-side NIM sensitivity vs peers

title: Falabella — Resultados Q4 2024
summary: Q4 2024 revenue +4.8% YoY; EBITDA -2.1% YoY; net income -18.5% YoY. Mixed result — FX headwinds in region.
aiSummary: Mixed quarter with revenue recovery offset by margin pressure. Chile same-store sales +3.5% is constructive, but regional FX depreciation (ARS, COP) hit consolidated margins. E-commerce remained a drag on profitability despite growing GMV. EBITDA margin compressed to 12% — still meaningfully below the 14-16% range the business can generate in a normalized environment. New CFO (effective April 2025) may bring renewed focus on cost discipline. The key investment thesis remains: can Falabella restore margins while managing the e-commerce profitability ramp? Execution risk is elevated.
keyPoints: Revenue: CLP 2,850,000MM (+4.8% YoY); Chile SSS +3.5%
EBITDA margin 12.0% (vs 12.8% prior year) — FX and cost pressure
Net income -18.5% YoY; below consensus on higher FX losses
E-commerce profitability path and new CFO strategy are key Q1 2025 catalysts

title: Copec — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +6.1% YoY; EBITDA +12.4% YoY; net income +18.2% YoY. Clean — fuel and Arauco both contributed.
aiSummary: Clean quarter with broad-based strength. Fuel distribution margins held firm in Chile and international markets; Arauco (forestry/panels) contributed positively on improved timber and pulp pricing vs prior year. Net income grew 18.2% YoY — strong in absolute terms. The Norwegian aquaculture acquisition (Blumar) adds a new earnings stream but integration costs will appear in future quarters. Copec is a quality business with diversified exposure to Chilean energy demand and global forest products. Key near-term question: how does the Blumar integration impact consolidated margins in Q2-Q3 2025?
keyPoints: EBITDA: CLP 480,000MM (+12.4% YoY); margin 11.4% (+0.6pp)
Fuel distribution: stable margins in Chile and international ops
Arauco forestry: improved timber pricing vs prior year
Watch: Blumar (Norway) integration costs and capex in Q2-Q3 2025

title: Enel Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +12.8% YoY; EBITDA +18.5% YoY; net income +22.4% YoY. EBITDA margin 36% — best-in-class.
aiSummary: Excellent quarter driven by renewables growth and hydrology recovery. Enel Chile's 36% EBITDA margin is best-in-class for Chilean utilities. Solar and wind additions drove revenue growth; improved hydrology (after a dry 2024) boosted output from hydroelectric assets. The 22.4% net income growth is impressive and ahead of consensus. Regulatory risk remains the primary overhang — the tariff review process could reset generation pricing in H2 2025. New renewable capacity commissioning schedule is also key. Enel Chile is the cleanest ESG-aligned utility play in Chile's equity market.
keyPoints: EBITDA margin: 36.0% (+1.8pp) — best-in-class for Chilean utilities
Net income: CLP 92,000MM (+22.4% YoY) — ahead of consensus
Drivers: solar/wind additions + hydrology normalization from dry 2024
Key risk: tariff review outcome (H2 2025) and commissioning delays on new capacity

title: LATAM Airlines — Resultados Q4 2024
summary: Q4 2024 revenue +9.2% YoY; EBITDA +14.1% YoY; net income +28.5% YoY. Strong demand + lower fuel = margin expansion.
aiSummary: Impressive quarter — best post-restructuring result to date. All traffic corridors showed strong demand; capacity +8% YoY. Lower Brent oil prices materially reduced fuel cost per ASK, driving EBITDAR margin to 18.1% — top of the 17-19% guidance range. Balance sheet has normalized post-Chapter 11; net debt/EBITDA approaching target leverage. The stock has re-rated significantly from restructuring levels. Going forward, key risks are cargo yield normalization and fleet renewal capex. Maintain watch on fuel hedging strategy for 2025.
keyPoints: EBITDAR margin 18.1% — top of guidance range; net income +28.5% YoY
Capacity +8% YoY; strong yield in all corridors
Fuel cost tailwind: lower Brent + favorable hedging
Balance sheet normalized; leverage trending toward mid-cycle target
Watch: cargo yield normalization and fleet renewal capex in 2025

title: Banco de Chile — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +6.8% YoY; net income +9.2% YoY. ROE >25% maintained. NII growth offset higher provisions.
aiSummary: Best-in-class ROE maintained above 25% — Banco de Chile continues to outperform sector peers on profitability. Loan portfolio grew 5% YoY with UF-indexed book benefiting from residual inflation pass-through. Higher provision expense (+15% YoY) reflects conservative coverage ratios in SME segment ahead of expected credit normalization. NIM was stable QoQ, which is positive given the rate environment. Banco de Chile's premium over sector (typically 15-20% P/BV premium) appears justified by ROE consistency. Watch asset quality deterioration in SME and mortgage book as economic recovery is still uneven.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 188,000MM (+9.2% YoY); ROE >25% maintained
Loan growth +5% YoY; UF-indexed portfolio benefits from CPI pass-through
Provision expense +15% YoY — conservative approach in SME segment
Premium valuation justified by consistent ROE delivery; watch SME credit quality

title: BCI — Resultados Q1 2025
summary: Q1 2025 revenue +5.1% YoY; net income +7.8% YoY. City National Bank (US) contributed positively.
aiSummary: Solid quarter, in line with expectations. BCI's differentiated asset — City National Bank (CNB) in the US — contributed positively, providing geographic diversification not seen in local peers. Chilean corporate loan book growing at 5%+ driven by infrastructure and mining projects. Controlled credit costs with no significant spike in NPLs. Key risk for BCI is CNB's exposure to US credit cycle: if Fed keeps rates high longer, commercial real estate and tech lending at CNB face headwinds. BCI trades at a discount to Santander Chile and Banco de Chile — narrowing of that discount is the re-rating catalyst if CNB performs well.
keyPoints: Net income: CLP 155,000MM (+7.8% YoY); in line with consensus
City National Bank (US) contribution positive — geographic diversification
Chile corporate loan book +5% driven by infrastructure/mining
Key risk: CNB exposure to US credit cycle; watch commercial real estate
